mnemonics.title: Exec Function Suffixes
mnemonics.content: P - PATH search, E - Environment, V - Vector/array, L - List format. Example: execvpe = Vector + PATH + Environment

mnemonics.title: IPC Categories
mnemonics.content: IPC = Communication + Synchronization. Comm: Pipe, MsgQ, Socket, SharedMem. Sync: Semaphore, Mutex, Signal. Think 'PMS' - Pipes, Messages, Sockets for data; 'SSS' for sync

mnemonics.title: Shared Memory Speed
mnemonics.content: Shared Memory is FAST - Direct access without copying. No kernel involvement for data transfer = Zero Copy.

mnemonics.title: FIFO Order
mnemonics.content: FIFO = First In First Out - like a queue. Named pipe in Unix.

acronyms.usage: Mechanisms for processes to exchange data and synchronize
Named pipe that preserves message order
Replace current program with new program in same process
Traditional Unix IPC: message queues, semaphores, shared memory
Standard API for Unix-like systems including IPC

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a running train (process) stopping at a station and all its passengers (program code/data) being replaced while the train itself (process ID) stays the same
Think of a water pipe - data flows in one direction only from one end to another. You need two pipes for two-way flow like hot and cold water lines
Multiple people writing in the same notebook - fast but need semaphore (lock) to prevent writing simultaneously
Traffic signal controls access to intersection (resource) - green means go (proceed), red means wait. Counter tracks available slots

analogies.analogy: Like a person putting on a different outfit - the person (process) remains the same, but what they wear (program) changes completely
Pipe is like speaking through a paper cup string - simple, local only. Socket is like a phone - can connect to anywhere locally or over network
Message queue is like postal mail - kernel delivers messages. Shared memory is like a shared whiteboard - both can read/write directly without delivery
Signals are like emergency alarms or phone calls - asynchronous notifications that interrupt whatever the process is doing
